prompt: Classify the following Cantina closure rationale into one of: dupe-of-prior, designed-as-intended, oos-clause, no-rubric-row, dos-class, severity-walkback, accepted-fix-in-progress. Closure text: 'Closed as duplicate of #N. Same root cause + same fix.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'Designed-as-intended; the team accepts this as a known trade-off documented in SECURITY.md.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'Out of scope per SCOPE.md: testnet-only deployment.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'No matching row in SEVERITY.md for this impact class. Rejected as no-rubric-row.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'DoS-class without proven non-DoS production impact; closed.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'Severity walked back from High to Low; defense-in-depth covers the bypass.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'Fix already in flight via PR #N at upstream repo. Marked accepted.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'Compromise of trusted infrastructure (sequencer) is OOS per program rules.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'Module x/feegrant is non-core for this program; rejected on R56 grounds.'
Classify the closure rationale: 'Already acknowledged in SRL-6.10 risk-accepted catalog.'
